solutions.solution: The analysis table is constructed as follows: [table with checkmarks and x marks shown for questions 11-20]. Answer combinations: a+b+c+d or a+(f)+c+d or (e)+b+c+d ⟹ answer 1, a+b+c+d ⟹ answer 4, a+b+(d)+d ⟹ answer 5, not to be given ⟹ answer 2, data inadequate ⟹ answer 3. The following answers are obtained by looking at the analysis table and the answer combinations: 11.2, 12.2, 13.3, 14.2, 15.5, 16.1, 17.1, 18.2, 19.3, 20.5

Please note that (ii) in itself is a condition attached to both d and e. If either (or both) of the two is violated, (i) becomes false. Therefore, for consequences see break up into two parts (a) and (b). [table with checkmarks and x marks shown for questions 21-30]. Answer combinations: a+b+c+d+e ⟹ answer 3, (b)+b+c+d+e ⟹ answer 4, a+b+c+d+(f), a+a+b+c+(a)+e, a+a+b+c+(a)+(b) ⟹ answer 5, not to be selected ⟹ answer 2, data inadequate ⟹ answer 1, any answer combinations are: 21.2, 22.3, 23.5, 24.1, 25.2, 26.1, 27.3, 28.2, 29.1, 30.1
